description: C0026 - ANDROMEDA-aligned collection and staged exfiltration chain adapted to a Linux lab substrate
objective: Reproduce the legacy six-technique C0026 chain with VM-backed collection, archiving, chunking, and controlled infrastructure staging.

techniques.name: Domains
techniques.description: For C0026, the threat actors re-registered expired C2 domains previously used for ANDROMEDA malware.
techniques.procedure_summary: Model re-registration of previously used ANDROMEDA C2 domains through a controlled infrastructure artifact.

techniques.name: Dynamic Resolution
techniques.description: During C0026, the threat actors re-registered a ClouDNS dynamic DNS subdomain which was previously used by ANDROMEDA.
techniques.procedure_summary: Resolve a lab-only dynamic domain on the target VM to preserve the observable resolution behavior without external DNS dependence.

techniques.name: Ingress Tool Transfer
techniques.description: During C0026, the threat actors downloaded malicious payloads onto select compromised hosts.
techniques.procedure_summary: Transfer a benign payload over HTTP inside the current execution substrate to preserve ingress tool transfer behavior.

techniques.name: Data from Local System
techniques.description: During C0026, the threat actors collected documents from compromised hosts.
techniques.procedure_summary: Collect benign host-local files from the target substrate into a constrained evidence bundle.

techniques.name: Archive via Utility
techniques.description: During C0026, the threat actors used WinRAR to collect documents on targeted systems and only exfiltrated files created after January 1, 2021.
techniques.procedure_summary: Archive collected benign data with native utilities on the target substrate before the exfiltration stage.

techniques.name: Data Transfer Size Limits
techniques.description: During C0026, the threat actors split encrypted archives containing stolen files and information into 3MB parts prior to exfiltration.
techniques.procedure_summary: Split the archive into bounded chunks to preserve the exfiltration pattern without sending sensitive content outside the lab.
